title: MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations
summary: The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA) , in partnership with the Wits Centre for Journalism (WCJ) in South Africa, has officially opened applications for its 2026 Training Workshop on Critical Coverage of Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations . This fully funded capacity-building programme is designed for journalists from selected Anglophone West African countries who are interested in strengthening their reporting on one of the most important global relationships shaping Africa s future. Scheduled to take place in Accra, Ghana , during the last quarter of 2026, the three-day workshop will bring together journalists from across the region to deepen their understanding of the complex political, economic, and social dimensions of Africa–China relations. The organisers aim to equip media professionals with the knowledge, analytical skills, and investigative techniques required to produce balanced, evidence-based, and impactful journalism that serves the public interest. Application Deadline: July 30, 2026 About the MFWA Training Workshop Africa s relationship with China has expanded significantly over the past two decades. China has become one of Africa s largest trading partners and investors, financing major infrastructure projects, supporting industrial development, and increasing cooperation in technology, mining, agriculture, energy, and manufacturing. However, this growing relationship also raises important questions surrounding: Trade and economic partnerships Infrastructure development Development financing Natural resource extraction Labour practices Technology transfer Environmental sustainability Governance and accountability International diplomacy Public policy As geopolitical dynamics continue to evolve, journalists play a critical role in helping citizens understand the opportunities, challenges, and implications of Africa–China cooperation. Reporting on these issues requires more than simply covering official announcements—it demands context, critical analysis, investigative skills, and an understanding of international affairs. Recognising this need, the Media Foundation for West Africa has partnered with the Wits Centre for Journalism to organise this specialised workshop titled Building Capacity to Cover a Complex Story – Africa and China Relations. The programme is funded by the University of the Witwatersrand and is intended to strengthen the reporting capacity of journalists across Anglophone West Africa. What Participants Will Learn The workshop combines subject-matter expertise with practical journalism skills. Participants will gain insights into: Understanding the evolving Africa–China relationship Reporting on international trade and investment Analysing development financing and infrastructure projects Investigating governance and accountability issues Covering natural resource extraction responsibly Reporting on labour, technology, and environmental concerns Applying investigative journalism techniques Producing balanced, accurate, and evidence-based stories Framing stories from African perspectives Understanding global geopolitical shifts and their implications for African countries The training seeks to improve both the depth and quality of reporting on international affairs while encouraging journalism that promotes transparency and informed public debate. Benefits of the Programme Successful applicants will receive comprehensive financial support throughout the programme. The organisers will cover: Return economy-class airfare Accommodation Meals Local transportation within Ghana This ensures participants can focus entirely on learning and networking without worrying about travel expenses. Additional Opportunities After the Training One of the most exciting aspects of this programme is that participation does not end after the workshop. Graduates will have the opportunity to apply for: Reporting grants to produce in-depth investigative stories on Africa–China relations. Funding support for publishing high-quality public-interest journalism. Professional mentorship and continued engagement with experts. Furthermore, three outstanding participants will be selected for a fully funded opportunity to attend the Africa Investigative Journalism Conference (AIJC) , scheduled for 10–12 November 2026 in Kenya , where they will present and discuss their reporting projects. Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Be based in one of the eligible Anglophone West African countries. Work in print, radio, television, or digital journalism. Report on one or more of the following beats: Politics Economy Business Trade Development Environment Extractive industries Infrastructure International affairs Be available during September–October 2026. Obtain a formal permission letter from their editor allowing participation. Be capable of producing an in-depth story after completing the workshop. Why Journalists Should Apply Today s journalists increasingly cover stories that extend beyond national borders. International trade agreements, foreign investments, infrastructure financing, and geopolitical developments directly affect local communities and national development. This workshop provides a unique opportunity to strengthen professional skills while learning from leading experts in journalism and international affairs. Participants will also build valuable regional networks with fellow journalists, exchange experiences, and gain practical tools for producing investigative stories that inform public debate and promote accountability. For journalists interested in politics, economics, business reporting, development journalism, or investigative reporting, this programme represents an important opportunity for career development and professional growth. How to Apply Interested journalists should complete the official online application form provided by the Media Foundation for West Africa. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications carefully and ensure they meet every eligibility requirement before submitting. Application Deadline: July 30, 2026. For more information and to submit your application, visit the official MFWA application page: Discover more global workshop opportunities on OFY here The post MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide
summary: Researchers making groundbreaking contributions to the fight against cancer now have the opportunity to gain one of the world s most prestigious recognitions in oncology. The Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 has officially opened applications, inviting outstanding cancer researchers from across the globe to compete for an award valued at €200,000 . Established by the Fondation Gustave Roussy , with the support of Laboratoires Pierre Fabre as its founding sponsor, the prize recognizes researchers whose scientific discoveries have significantly transformed cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care. Since its launch in 2025, the award has rapidly become one of the most highly respected international distinctions in oncology research, celebrating scientific excellence and innovations that deliver measurable and lasting benefits for cancer patients worldwide. Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST About the Gustave Roussy Prize Cancer remains one of the world s leading health challenges, affecting millions of people every year. Continuous scientific research has resulted in remarkable progress in early diagnosis, precision medicine, immunotherapy, targeted therapies, prevention strategies, and patient-centered care. The Gustave Roussy Prize was created to recognize researchers whose discoveries have contributed substantially to these advances. The award was launched in 2025 to commemorate two important milestones: The 150th anniversary of Professor Gustave Roussy s birth. The centenary of the Gustave Roussy Institute. Professor Gustave Roussy is widely recognized as a pioneer in cancer care. In 1921, he established one of the world s first medical consultations dedicated specifically to patients with tumors, laying the foundation for modern comprehensive oncology care. Today, Gustave Roussy is recognized as Europe s leading cancer center and continues to drive innovation through cutting-edge research, education, and patient care. Prize Value The Gustave Roussy Prize carries a total value of €200,000 , making it one of the largest international awards dedicated to cancer research. The funding is divided into two components: €100,000 awarded directly to the laureate for unrestricted personal use. €100,000 allocated to support an innovative oncology research project, enabling continued scientific discovery and future breakthroughs. Purpose of the Award The prize celebrates scientific breakthroughs that have created tangible improvements in cancer care and patient outcomes. Research achievements may relate to: Cancer diagnosis Cancer treatment Cancer prevention Clinical oncology Translational medicine Precision medicine Immunotherapy Biomarker discovery Screening technologies Therapeutic innovation Improved quality of patient care Better understanding of cancer biology The emphasis is not simply on academic achievement but on research that demonstrates measurable clinical impact and has improved the lives of patients. Who Can Apply? Applications are welcomed from researchers worldwide regardless of nationality or institutional affiliation. Eligible candidates include researchers working at: Universities Cancer research institutes Medical schools Hospitals Public research organizations Private research institutions International research centers Applicants are expected to demonstrate exceptional scientific excellence and a research record that has significantly advanced oncology. Selection Process Applications will be evaluated by an independent international jury composed of leading experts in oncology and biomedical research. Selection will be based exclusively on: Scientific excellence Originality of discoveries Clinical impact Innovation Long-term contribution to oncology Demonstrated benefits for patients Importantly, evaluations are conducted without regard to discipline, institution, or nationality, ensuring a fair and merit-based selection process. Why This Prize Matters Receiving the Gustave Roussy Prize represents more than financial recognition. It places recipients among the world s leading oncology researchers and highlights discoveries that have changed the landscape of cancer care. The prize also encourages continued innovation by providing dedicated funding for future research projects, ensuring that today s breakthroughs continue generating tomorrow s solutions. With cancer affecting millions globally, supporting transformative research remains essential for improving survival rates, enhancing quality of life, and developing more effective treatments. This award reinforces the importance of scientific collaboration and recognizes researchers whose work creates lasting benefits for humanity. Previous Laureates The prize has already honored internationally renowned scientists. Previous recipients include: 2025: Professor Charles Swanton 2026: Professor Luis Diaz Jr. These researchers were recognized for discoveries that have significantly influenced modern oncology and patient treatment. Award Ceremony The selected laureate for the 2027 edition will receive the prize on February 4, 2027 , during World Cancer Day , highlighting the global importance of cancer research and international collaboration in combating the disease. How to Apply Researchers interested in the Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 should complete the official online application before the deadline. Applicants are encouraged to present their most impactful scientific achievements, clearly demonstrate clinical relevance, and provide evidence showing how their discoveries have advanced cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care. Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST. Interested applicants can find more information through the official Gustave Roussy website Discover more global awards opportunities on OFY here The post Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience
summary: The Aspen Institute s Religion Society Program is now accepting applications for the Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 , an exclusive professional development opportunity designed for emerging journalists who want to deepen their understanding of the role religion plays in shaping politics, culture, public policy, and civic life. Taking place in Middleburg, Virginia, USA , from August 30 to September 1, 2026 , the Forum provides an intellectually engaging environment where early-career journalists can interact with experienced reporters, editors, scholars, and thought leaders through meaningful conversations rooted in the Aspen Institute s long-standing tradition of respectful dialogue and critical thinking. The Michael Cromartie Forum is more than a journalism conference—it is an immersive learning experience that equips participants with new perspectives, stronger reporting skills, and valuable professional networks that can influence their careers for years to come. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and are expected to close around July 1, 2026. Early applications are strongly encouraged. About the Michael Cromartie Forum In today s rapidly evolving media landscape, journalists are expected to cover increasingly complex issues that intersect with politics, religion, identity, ethics, democracy, and culture. Religion continues to influence elections, policymaking, international affairs, education, social movements, and community life, making religious literacy an essential skill for modern journalism. Recognizing this need, the Aspen Institute s Religion Society Program established the Michael Cromartie Forum to help emerging journalists better understand how faith and religion influence public life while strengthening their ability to report accurately, fairly, and thoughtfully on these issues. Named in honor of the late Michael Cromartie , a respected advocate for improving religion reporting in mainstream media, the Forum continues his legacy by creating space for journalists to engage with scholars and experienced practitioners in open, nonpartisan dialogue. The program emphasizes curiosity, intellectual humility, and rigorous discussion rather than debate or advocacy. What Participants Can Expect During the three-day Forum, participants will engage in a series of carefully curated discussions, presentations, and interactive sessions covering topics at the intersection of religion and public life. The program brings together: Experienced journalists from leading media organizations. Editors with expertise in religion, politics, and public affairs reporting. Distinguished scholars and researchers. Public intellectuals and subject-matter experts. Fellow emerging journalists from diverse backgrounds. Rather than focusing on breaking news, the Forum encourages participants to examine the deeper historical, cultural, philosophical, and ethical dimensions of current events. This format allows journalists to gain insights that strengthen their long-term reporting and storytelling abilities. Why You Should Apply Participating in the Michael Cromartie Forum offers numerous professional and personal benefits, including: Exposure to leading scholars and experienced journalists. A deeper understanding of religion s influence on politics, society, and culture. Opportunities to improve reporting on complex public issues. Networking with fellow journalists from different media organizations. Participation in thoughtful, nonpartisan discussions. Membership in a growing community of journalism professionals committed to intellectual excellence. Professional growth through meaningful dialogue and collaborative learning. The Forum provides participants with the time and space to think critically about journalism beyond the pressures of daily newsroom deadlines. Who Is the Forum Designed For? The program is primarily intended for emerging journalists between the ages of 22 and 30 , although the organizers clearly state that there are no strict age requirements . Applicants may include: Print journalists. Broadcast journalists. Digital media reporters. Multimedia journalists. Editors early in their careers. Independent journalists. Freelance reporters. Journalism graduates beginning their professional careers. The organizers welcome applicants who demonstrate intellectual curiosity, a commitment to high-quality journalism, and an interest in understanding the role religion plays in contemporary society. Venue and Dates The 2026 Michael Cromartie Forum will take place in: Location: Middleburg, Virginia, USA Dates: August 30 – September 1, 2026 The setting is intentionally designed to encourage focused learning, reflection, relationship-building, and open dialogue among participants. Why Religion Reporting Matters Religion remains one of the most influential forces shaping communities across the world. It affects public policy, elections, education, healthcare, international relations, social justice movements, humanitarian work, and cultural identity. Despite this influence, religion is often misunderstood or underreported in mainstream journalism. The Michael Cromartie Forum seeks to bridge this gap by helping journalists: Improve religious literacy. Better understand diverse faith traditions. Report accurately on religion-related issues. Explore how religious beliefs intersect with politics and public life. Produce more nuanced, balanced, and informed journalism. These skills are increasingly valuable in today s interconnected and multicultural world. Professional Networking Opportunities One of the Forum s greatest strengths is the opportunity to build lasting professional relationships. Participants become part of a network that includes: National and international journalists. Editors. Academic experts. Public policy specialists. Religion scholars. Media leaders. These connections often lead to future collaborations, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application through the Aspen Institute Online Form. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , meaning candidates may receive consideration as applications are submitted. Since spaces are limited, applying early significantly improves the chances of being considered. Expected Application Deadline: Around July 1, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare a thoughtful application that demonstrates their journalism experience, curiosity about public affairs, and commitment to producing accurate, insightful reporting. Discover more global conference opportunities on OFY here The post Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026: Birregional Training for Women Leaders in Local Politics Across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union
summary: The EU-LAC Foundation has officially launched the EU-LAC Birregional School of Local Political Leadership for Women 2026 , a high-impact leadership development programme designed to strengthen women’s political participation, advocacy skills, and leadership capacity at the local level across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union . This initiative is a strategic space for training, exchange, and networking , bringing together women leaders from different political, community, and social backgrounds. The programme is built on a feminist, intersectional, and human rights-based approach , aiming to empower women to lead democratic, inclusive, and transformative change within their communities. The School combines virtual preparatory sessions with a face-to-face intensive training in Panama , ensuring both accessibility and meaningful intercultural exchange among participants. Objective of the EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School The main goal of the programme is to strengthen the capacity of women engaged in territorial and local leadership by providing: Advanced training in political leadership and governance Practical tools for communication, negotiation, and public management Opportunities for intergenerational mentoring Strong birregional networking between Europe, Latin America, and the Caribbean A platform for sharing lived experiences and community-based leadership strategies Through this initiative, the EU-LAC Foundation aims to reinforce women’s leadership in democratic governance and civic participation at the local level . Format of the Leadership School The programme follows a blended learning structure , combining digital and in-person components: Virtual Preparatory Phase Online training modules before the in-person session Introduction to leadership concepts, tools, and frameworks Networking and group discussions across regions In-Person Intensive Session Held in Panama (October 2026) High-level training workshops Interactive leadership sessions Cross-regional experience sharing Networking with women leaders from EU-LAC regions Follow-Up Activities Continued engagement through networks Participation in the EU-LAC WIN Network Knowledge sharing and leadership development continuation Profile of Applicants The programme is designed for two main categories of women leaders: Young Women Leaders Applicants should demonstrate: Membership in a network, organisation, programme, or institution Interest in territorial political leadership (formal or informal) Engagement in civic participation, feminist groups, or community initiatives Motivation to strengthen leadership skills and collaborate with women across regions Interest in EU-LAC relations, gender equality, or local development Willingness to join or engage with the EU-LAC WIN Network Senior Women Leaders Applicants should demonstrate: Strong and consolidated leadership experience in political or community spaces Proven impact in local governance, municipalities, councils, or civil society initiatives Membership in a recognised institution, network, or organisation Capacity to mentor and support younger women leaders Commitment to feminist and intersectional leadership principles Active contribution to community transformation and policy influence Required Application Documents All applications must be submitted in English or Spanish and include: Signed application form Proof of membership in a network, organisation, or institution Letter of endorsement or support from a relevant institution or organisation Submission Format PDF or MS Word format only Incomplete or late applications will not be considered Key Benefits of the Programme Selected participants will gain access to a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities, including: High-level leadership training in political and civic engagement Strengthening negotiation, communication, and governance skills Participation in a birregional leadership network Exposure to feminist and intersectional leadership frameworks Opportunities for mentorship and intergenerational learning Exchange of experiences across Europe, Latin America, and the Caribbean Capacity building in local governance and advocacy Financial Support (Scholarships) For selected participants who cannot self-finance their participation, the EU-LAC Foundation will provide: International travel coverage Hotel accommodation Local transport in Panama Daily stipend for meals and subsistence during the in-person phase Application Deadline Friday, 24 July 2026 (23:59 CEST – Germany time) Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. How to Apply Applications must be submitted by email to: Email: eulacwin@eulacfoundation.org Attention: Dr Anna Barrera Subject Line: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 + Applicant’s Name Selection Process Communication Applications will be reviewed by an internal selection committee Evaluation period: approximately 3 weeks after the deadline Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted via email Applicants are advised not to send repeated inquiries during the review period All official updates will be communicated electronically Final Overview The EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 is a unique opportunity for women leaders to build transformative leadership skills, strengthen democratic participation, and join a powerful international network committed to gender equality and social change. By combining training, mentorship, and intercultural dialogue, the programme empowers women to become stronger voices in local governance and community transformation. Applications should be sent to the attention of Dr Anna Barrera (EU-LAC Foundation). Email: eulacwin@eulacfoundation.org Subject line: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 + Applicant’s Name Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026: Birregional Training for Women Leaders in Local Politics Across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) Opportunities at the University of Johannesburg: Global Excellence and Societal Impact (GES 4.0) Initiative
summary: The University of Johannesburg (UJ) , through its Global Excellence and Stature (GES 4.0) Social Impact Initiative , is inviting applications from highly qualified and early-career researchers for its Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) programme . This prestigious fellowship is designed to attract outstanding postdoctoral researchers who can contribute meaningfully to high-impact research, innovation, interdisciplinarity, and societal transformation. This fellowship is part of UJ’s long-term strategic vision (UJ Strategic Plan 2035), which positions the university as a globally competitive research institution addressing critical challenges facing society, the environment, governance systems, and technological transformation. The programme seeks exceptional scholars who demonstrate strong academic excellence, innovative thinking, and a commitment to solving real-world problems aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Overview of the Postdoctoral Research Fellowship Programme The PDRF at the University of Johannesburg is a full-time research fellowship that supports emerging scholars in conducting advanced research across priority thematic areas. Fellows will be embedded within recognized research entities, centres, institutes, SARChI Chairs, or strategic research programmes at UJ. This ensures that successful applicants are fully integrated into a vibrant academic environment that promotes collaboration, mentorship, and interdisciplinary research output. Priority Research Areas Applicants must align their research proposals with one or more of the following thematic areas: Sustainability, Climate Change, and Environmental Governance (ESG) Water, Energy, Food, and Environmental Security Public Health and Mental Health Systems Education and Child Development Migration, Social Justice, and Inequality Governance, Democracy, and Public Policy African Futures and Pan-African Thought Advanced Manufacturing and Industry 4.0 Smart Systems and Data Science Innovation, Entrepreneurship, and Societal Impact Artificial Intelligence and Digital Transformation These areas reflect UJ’s commitment to addressing global and African developmental challenges through cutting-edge research. Fellowship Conditions and Benefits Successful applicants will receive a highly competitive research support package designed to enable impactful academic work. Key Benefits Include: Competitive tax-free stipend of R250,000 per year Initial appointment for 12 months , renewable based on performance and funding availability Access to world-class UJ research infrastructure Integration into national and international research networks Mentorship from senior academics and research leaders Opportunities for professional development and academic growth The fellowship does not include relocation or accommodation support. Expectations of Postdoctoral Fellows Fellows are expected to contribute actively to the academic and research environment at UJ. Responsibilities include: Participation in capacity-building and research activities Co-supervision of postgraduate students Development of research funding proposals Presentation of research findings at conferences, seminars, and webinars Publishing research in peer-reviewed journals, books, or scholarly platforms Active participation in collaborative research networks Contribution to additional research initiatives aligned with host entities This ensures fellows not only conduct research but also contribute to the broader academic ecosystem. Eligibility and Minimum Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Academic Requirements Hold a PhD or equivalent doctoral qualification (NQF Level 10) PhD must have been obtained within the last five years Must not have held a previous postdoctoral fellowship at the host discipline (preferred condition) Research Competencies Strong scholarly and research performance record Excellent academic writing and communication skills Proven research output or publications Other Requirements Ability to work full-time for the duration of the fellowship Demonstrated interest in interdisciplinary research and societal impact Preference for candidates with international collaboration experience Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package including: Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV) Certified copies of academic qualifications Copy of doctoral thesis abstract Motivation letter outlining suitability for the fellowship Research proposal (3–5 pages) aligned to a host research area Two academic reference letters Evidence of publications or research outputs Application Process Applications must be submitted electronically to the designated application platform or contact person provided by the university. For enquiries: Contact: Mr Thabiso Masango Email: thabisom@uj.ac.za Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Value of the Fellowship The PDRF at UJ is not only a financial opportunity but also a career-defining academic experience. It provides: High-level research exposure in a globally recognized institution Access to interdisciplinary academic networks Opportunities to shape policy-relevant and impactful research Strong foundation for future academic or research leadership roles Application Deadline 15 July 2026 Conclusion The University of Johannesburg Postdoctoral Research Fellowship under the GES 4.0 Initiative represents one of the most competitive research opportunities in Africa. It is designed for emerging scholars who are ready to contribute to global knowledge production while addressing pressing societal challenges. Applicants with strong academic backgrounds, innovative research ideas, and interdisciplinary interests are strongly encouraged to apply. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) Opportunities at the University of Johannesburg: Global Excellence and Societal Impact (GES 4.0) Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Journalists, Editors, and Media Influencers to Attend a Three-Day Forum in Abuja, Nigeria
summary: The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID), through its Health Reporting Project, has officially opened applications for the African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026 , a fully funded three-day in-person forum that will bring together journalists, editors, and media influencers from across Africa to strengthen road safety journalism and improve how road traffic crashes are reported across the continent. This unique forum seeks to transform road safety reporting by equipping media professionals with practical skills, technical knowledge, and collaborative opportunities to produce impactful stories that focus on accountability, prevention, and evidence-based solutions rather than simply reporting road crashes as isolated incidents. The forum will be held in Abuja, Nigeria , in September 2026 , bringing together 15 carefully selected media professionals who will work alongside experts from the World Health Organization (WHO), leading African editors, and experienced journalists to develop industry-standard editorial guidelines for road safety reporting across Africa. If you are passionate about public health reporting, investigative journalism, development journalism, or road safety advocacy, this is an excellent opportunity to expand your professional skills while contributing to safer roads across Africa. About the African Road Safety Reporting Forum Road traffic crashes remain one of the world s most pressing public health challenges. Globally, road crashes are the leading cause of death among children and young people between the ages of 5 and 29 years . Even more concerning, road traffic deaths continue to rise more rapidly in Africa than in any other region of the world. Despite the enormous impact of road crashes, media reporting often fails to reflect the true scale of the crisis. Many news reports portray crashes as unfortunate accidents that simply happen, rather than highlighting the preventable systemic issues behind them, including: Poor road infrastructure Weak enforcement of road safety laws Policy failures Inadequate governance Unsafe transport systems Lack of accountability In addition, media coverage frequently focuses on blaming individual road users while overlooking broader structural causes that contribute to road traffic injuries and fatalities. The African Road Safety Reporting Forum aims to change this narrative by helping journalists produce stories that encourage policy reforms, accountability, and sustainable road safety solutions. Objectives of the Forum Participants will work together to: Develop industry-standard editorial guidelines for road safety reporting in Africa. Improve the quality and impact of road safety journalism. Produce compelling road safety stories using newly developed reporting approaches. Promote solutions-focused journalism. Encourage accountability-driven reporting. Strengthen collaboration among African media professionals working on public health and transport issues. Build a continent-wide network of journalists committed to improving road safety coverage. Who Should Apply? The forum is designed for professionals working within Africa s media landscape who are interested in improving public health and road safety reporting. Applicants should be: Journalists Editors Media influencers Applicants must also demonstrate a strong interest in: Public health journalism Road safety reporting Accountability journalism Development reporting Investigative reporting Although the forum particularly seeks participants from Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia, and South Africa , applications from other African countries are also welcomed. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Be based in Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia, South Africa, or another African country. Be an editor, journalist, or media influencer. Demonstrate an interest in public health or road safety reporting. Be available to attend the three-day physical forum in September 2026. Be willing to develop and publish a road safety story following the forum. Be willing to participate in an online working group that will continue developing African road safety reporting guidelines. Benefits of the African Road Safety Reporting Forum Successful applicants will receive several valuable benefits, including: 1. Practical Professional Training Participants will receive hands-on training in: Solutions-focused journalism Accountability reporting Effective road safety reporting Public health communication 2. Access to Leading Experts Participants will interact directly with: WHO technical experts Leading African editors Experienced journalists Road safety professionals 3. Influence Future Journalism Standards Participants will contribute to creating editorial guidelines that may influence road safety reporting across Africa. 4. Produce High-Impact Stories Participants will develop and publish road safety stories that reflect the newly developed editorial standards. 5. Fully Funded Participation Selected participants will receive: Full travel support Accommodation Daily stipend throughout the forum Why This Forum Matters Media plays a powerful role in shaping public understanding and influencing policy decisions. By improving how road traffic crashes are reported, journalists can help: Raise public awareness Encourage stronger government accountability Promote evidence-based road safety policies Influence positive behavioural change Reduce preventable road deaths across Africa This forum therefore represents not only a professional development opportunity but also a chance to contribute to meaningful public health improvements across the continent. Application Deadline Deadline: 10 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted, and successful applicants will receive notification via email. Final Thoughts The African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for African journalists, editors, and media influencers to improve their reporting skills while contributing to a safer future for millions of road users across Africa. Through expert-led training, collaboration with leading media professionals, and the development of continent-wide editorial standards, participants will play a significant role in transforming road safety journalism from reactive reporting into impactful storytelling that promotes accountability and lasting policy change. Interested applicants should prepare their submissions early and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements before the application deadline of 10 July 2026 . Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Journalists, Editors, and Media Influencers to Attend a Three-Day Forum in Abuja, Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28 Now Open: Fully Funded Government of Ireland Master’s Scholarships for Emerging Leaders from Ghana and Nigeria
summary: Applications are now officially open for the Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28 , one of the Government of Ireland s flagship fully funded international scholarship programmes for emerging leaders from developing countries. If you are an ambitious professional from Ghana or Nigeria with leadership potential and a passion for driving sustainable development in your community, this is your opportunity to earn a fully funded master s degree at one of Ireland s world-renowned universities. Funded by the Government of Ireland through the Department of Foreign Affairs and implemented with support from the Irish Council for International Students (ICOS) , the Ireland Fellows Programme is designed to empower future leaders with advanced academic knowledge, professional skills, international networks, and lifelong connections with Ireland. The programme covers full tuition fees, international flights, accommodation support, and a monthly living stipend, allowing selected fellows to focus entirely on their studies and leadership development. With applications currently open for the 2027/28 academic year , eligible professionals from Ghana and Nigeria are encouraged to begin preparing their applications as early as possible before the deadline. About the Ireland Fellows Programme The Ireland Fellows Programme is a prestigious international scholarship initiative established by the Government of Ireland to strengthen leadership capacity and support sustainable development across partner countries. The programme provides talented early- and mid-career professionals with the opportunity to pursue master s level education in Ireland while developing the skills, knowledge, and global networks needed to become influential leaders in government, academia, civil society, business, and international development. More than simply funding postgraduate education, the programme invests in people who have demonstrated the potential to contribute meaningfully to national development upon returning to their home countries. By bringing together future leaders from partner countries, the programme also promotes stronger international cooperation and long-term relationships between Ireland and participating nations. Applications Are Now Open Applications for the Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28 are currently open. Eligible applicants from Ghana and Nigeria should carefully review the country-specific application guidance before submitting their Stage 1 application. Interested applicants are encouraged to begin the application process early to allow sufficient time to gather the required documents and confirm their eligibility. What Does the Scholarship Cover? The Ireland Fellows Programme offers a comprehensive financial package that removes many of the barriers associated with studying abroad. Successful applicants will receive: Full Tuition Fees The programme covers the entire tuition cost for an approved master s programme at a participating higher education institution in Ireland. Return International Flights Recipients receive funding for return international airfare between their home country and Ireland. Accommodation Support Financial support is provided to assist with accommodation costs throughout the study period. Monthly Living Allowance Participants receive a monthly stipend to cover day-to-day living expenses, including food, transportation, utilities, and other personal costs while studying in Ireland. This generous funding package enables fellows to fully concentrate on their academic and professional development without the financial pressures often associated with postgraduate study overseas. Why Study in Ireland? Ireland has become one of Europe s leading destinations for international education, attracting students from across the world through its globally recognized universities and welcoming academic environment. Studying in Ireland offers numerous advantages, including: Internationally Recognized Universities Ireland s universities are renowned for academic excellence, innovative teaching, and research-led learning across a wide range of disciplines. Strong Career Prospects Graduates from Irish institutions benefit from internationally respected qualifications that enhance career opportunities in government, international organizations, private industry, research institutions, and civil society organizations. Global Professional Networks Participants have the opportunity to build lifelong relationships with fellow professionals, academics, policymakers, and development practitioners from around the world. Research and Innovation Ireland is recognized as a global leader in research, innovation, technology, healthcare, agriculture, sustainability, and entrepreneurship, providing an excellent environment for postgraduate education. Leadership for Sustainable Development The Ireland Fellows Programme places leadership at the heart of its mission. Rather than supporting education alone, the programme seeks individuals who are committed to using their newly acquired knowledge and skills to strengthen institutions, improve communities, influence public policy, and contribute to sustainable development in their home countries. Participants are expected to become ambassadors of positive change, applying their education to solve real-world challenges and promote inclusive development. Supporting the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) The programme aligns closely with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Successful fellows are encouraged to pursue academic pathways that contribute to areas such as: Quality Education Gender Equality Good Health and Well-being Climate Action Innovation and Infrastructure Decent Work and Economic Growth Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions Partnerships for the Goals Through these areas, the programme contributes to strengthening sustainable development both locally and globally. Eligible Countries Applications are currently open for eligible applicants from: Ghana Applicants from Ghana should review the official programme guidance, confirm eligibility requirements, and select an approved master s programme before submitting their application. Nigeria Applicants from Nigeria should carefully review the programme guidelines, verify their eligibility, and complete the required application steps before the deadline. Applicants must apply through the country corresponding to both their nationality and current residence. Who Should Apply? The programme is designed for professionals who have already demonstrated commitment and leadership within their careers and communities. Strong applicants typically demonstrate: Leadership potential. Professional achievement. Commitment to public service or community development. Desire to contribute to sustainable development. Interest in international collaboration. Motivation to build long-term partnerships between Ireland and their home country. The programme seeks individuals capable of creating meaningful and lasting impact after completing their studies. Application Process Applications follow a structured, competitive, multi-stage process. Stage 1: Preliminary Application Applicants submit their initial application, providing personal information and confirming eligibility. Stage 2: Detailed Application Shortlisted candidates complete a comprehensive application including: Academic background. Employment history. Supporting documents. Master s programme preferences. Personal motivation. Leadership achievements. Stage 3: Interview and Final Assessment Candidates progressing to the final stage may be invited to interviews and other assessment activities before final scholarship offers are made. How to Apply Applicants should complete the following steps: Visit the official application page for Ghana or Nigeria. Carefully review the application guidance and eligibility criteria. Select an eligible master s programme. Prepare all required documents. Submit the Stage 1 application before the deadline. Wait for notification regarding shortlisting and further assessment. Applicants should ensure that every section of their application is complete and accurate before submission. APPLY HERE Why This Scholarship Stands Out The Ireland Fellows Programme is widely regarded as one of the most prestigious government-funded scholarship opportunities available to professionals from partner countries. Major benefits include: Fully funded master s education. Full tuition coverage. Return international flights. Accommodation support. Monthly living stipend. Leadership development. Professional networking opportunities. Exposure to Ireland s world-class higher education system. Long-term connections with Ireland and global development partners. Opportunity to contribute to sustainable national development upon graduation. Application Deadline Application Deadline: 26 July 2026 Eligible applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their applications immediately. Early preparation allows sufficient time to gather supporting documents, review eligible master s programmes, and complete every stage of the application carefully. Visit HERE to learn more about the Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28. Conclusion The Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28 offers an exceptional opportunity for ambitious professionals from Ghana and Nigeria to pursue fully funded master s studies in Ireland while developing the leadership skills necessary to create lasting impact in their countries. With applications now officially open, this is the ideal time to begin preparing your application. Beyond earning an internationally recognized postgraduate qualification, successful fellows will join a global network of development leaders committed to strengthening institutions, advancing sustainable development, and building enduring partnerships between Ireland and Africa. If you aspire to become a transformational leader in your profession and community, the Ireland Fellows Programme 2027/28 could be the opportunity that shapes the next chapter of your career. Review the eligibility requirements, prepare your application carefully, and submit it before the deadline to maximize your chances of success. Discover more global Scholarship and Fellowship opportunities on OFY. title: IFC SME Growth Accelerator 2026: Up to USD 1.5 Million in Grant Funding for High-Potential SMEs in Emerging Markets
summary: High-potential small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) operating in emerging markets have an exciting opportunity to accelerate their business growth through the IFC SME Growth Accelerator 2026 . The programme offers grant funding of up to USD 1.5 million , alongside mentorship, business networking opportunities, and potential follow-on financing to help ambitious businesses expand their operations, enter new markets, and create quality employment. The SME Growth Accelerator is an initiative of the International Finance Corporation (IFC) , a member of the World Bank Group dedicated to supporting private sector development in emerging economies. The programme is designed to help promising SMEs overcome growth barriers by providing financial support and strategic business development resources. If your enterprise is looking to scale its impact, strengthen its competitive advantage, or expand into regional or international markets, this funding opportunity could provide the resources needed to achieve your growth ambitions. About the IFC SME Growth Accelerator Small and medium-sized enterprises are the backbone of many developing economies, contributing significantly to employment, innovation, and economic growth. However, many SMEs struggle to access the financing and technical support required to expand successfully. The IFC SME Growth Accelerator seeks to bridge this gap by supporting businesses with grant funding and complementary business development services. Beyond financial assistance, the programme aims to strengthen the long-term sustainability and competitiveness of participating enterprises. Successful applicants may also benefit from mentorship, expanded professional networks, and opportunities for future investment. Funding Available Eligible SMEs may receive up to USD 1.5 million in grant funding to support business growth and expansion activities. The exact amount awarded may depend on the nature of the proposed project, its scalability, expected development impact, and the applicant s overall funding needs. What Can the Grant Be Used For? The programme supports a broad range of business development activities. Eligible expenditures include: Working capital to support business operations. Pilot deployments for testing and validating innovative products or services. Technical assistance and capacity-building activities. Climate-related investments. Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives. Market research to identify new business opportunities. Feasibility studies for expansion projects. Equipment procurement. Technology acquisition and implementation. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) activities. Activities that strengthen operational efficiency and long-term sustainability. These eligible expenditures are intended to help SMEs build stronger businesses while increasing their economic and social impact. Additional Benefits of the Programme Beyond grant funding, successful applicants may gain access to several valuable opportunities, including: Mentorship from experienced business professionals. Access to regional and international business networks. Potential follow-on financing opportunities. Increased visibility among investors and development partners. Support in strengthening investment readiness. Opportunities to improve organizational capacity and business performance. These additional benefits can help businesses position themselves for sustainable growth beyond the grant period. Who Can Apply? The SME Growth Accelerator targets high-potential Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs) that are ready to scale their operations and demonstrate strong growth potential. Applicants should be able to demonstrate: A legally established SME. Strong growth ambitions. A scalable business model. Capacity to expand into new markets. Potential to create quality employment opportunities. Readiness to effectively manage grant funding. Commitment to delivering measurable business and development outcomes. Applicants are encouraged to review the official programme guidelines to confirm all eligibility requirements before applying. Eligible Regions The programme is open to eligible SMEs operating in the following regions: Africa Asia Latin America The Caribbean Businesses located within these regions are encouraged to explore the opportunity if they meet the programme s eligibility criteria. Application Process Interested SMEs should prepare a comprehensive proposal outlining: Their business model. Growth strategy. Market expansion plans. Expected development impact. Job creation potential. Budget and funding requirements. Implementation timeline. Monitoring and evaluation approach. A well-prepared proposal that clearly demonstrates scalability, financial readiness, and measurable impact is likely to be more competitive. Click here for more and to apply Consulting Support The programme information indicates that consulting services are available to assist SMEs in preparing investment-ready funding proposals aligned with international evaluation standards. These services may include support in: Strategic planning. Results-Based Management (RBM). Business development planning. Defining measurable outcomes. Strengthening implementation frameworks. Monitoring and evaluation planning. Sustainability planning. Developing evidence-based funding applications. Such support can help businesses submit stronger and more competitive applications. Important Dates Application Deadline 15 November 2026 Additional timelines mentioned include: Request for Quotation Deadline: Rolling, subject to availability. Confirmation of Consulting Services Deadline: Rolling, subject to availability. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid delays. Tips for a Strong Application To improve your chances of success: Present a clear and realistic business growth strategy. Demonstrate market demand for your products or services. Highlight expected job creation. Explain how funding will accelerate business growth. Include measurable performance indicators. Show financial sustainability beyond the grant period. Demonstrate organizational capacity to implement the proposed activities. Provide realistic budgets supported by evidence. Why This Opportunity Matters Access to growth capital remains one of the biggest challenges facing SMEs in emerging markets. The IFC SME Growth Accelerator provides more than financial support—it offers businesses the opportunity to strengthen their operational capacity, expand into new markets, improve investment readiness, and create lasting economic impact. For ambitious SMEs seeking to scale sustainably while contributing to employment and economic development, this programme represents a valuable opportunity to accelerate long-term growth. Deadline Application Deadline: 15 November 2026 Click here for more global grant opportunities The post IFC SME Growth Accelerator 2026: Up to USD 1.5 Million in Grant Funding for High-Potential SMEs in Emerging Markets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: British Council TNE Grants with India 2026–27: £30,000 Funding for UK–India Higher Education Partnerships in Emerging Technologies
summary: The British Council has officially launched the highly anticipated TNE Grants with India 2026–27 under the Going Global Partnerships initiative, offering an exciting opportunity for UK and Indian higher education institutions to collaborate on innovative transnational education (TNE) programmes in cutting-edge disciplines. This international grant programme is designed to strengthen UK–India higher education collaboration by supporting the development and launch of new TNE courses and degree programmes in future-focused sectors that are shaping the global economy. With funding of up to £30,000 available per project, the initiative aims to encourage interdisciplinary academic innovation, academia-industry engagement, and workforce readiness in emerging technologies and scientific fields. What Are the TNE Grants with India 2026–27? The TNE Grants with India programme supports collaborative partnerships between UK universities, Indian higher education institutions, and industry partners to co-develop impactful educational programmes aligned with future industry demands. The grants are part of the British Council’s broader Going Global Partnerships programme, which promotes international collaboration in higher education, skills, and research. The initiative focuses specifically on developing innovative transnational education models that can enhance student mobility, academic excellence, employability, and cross-border institutional partnerships. Funding Details Selected partnerships will receive: Grant amount: £30,000 Project duration: 17 months Project start date: 30 September 2026 The funding is intended to support the planning, development, and launch of new TNE courses and academic programmes in strategic emerging areas. Priority Areas of Focus Applications are invited for projects connected to the following high-priority disciplines and innovation sectors: 1. AI and Machine Learning and Quantum Computing Projects focused on artificial intelligence, advanced machine learning systems, data science, quantum technologies, and computational innovation. 2. Genomics and Proteomics CRISPR-Based Therapies Programmes exploring genomics, biotechnology, genetic engineering, precision medicine, and CRISPR therapeutic applications. 3. Sustainable Energy and Climate Change Technology Courses addressing renewable energy, sustainability, green transition technologies, climate adaptation, and environmental resilience. 4. Synthetic Biology and Neurotechnology Innovative interdisciplinary programmes combining biology, neuroscience, engineering, and advanced medical technologies. 5. Circular Economy-Based Research Initiatives supporting sustainability, waste reduction, resource efficiency, recycling systems, and circular economic models. 6. Advanced Engineering, Robotics, and Space Technologies Programmes connected to robotics, aerospace innovation, automation, advanced manufacturing, and engineering technologies. 7. Semiconductor Technologies Projects focusing on semiconductor manufacturing, chip technologies, electronics innovation, and advanced computing infrastructure. 8. Critical Minerals, Geological and Earth Sciences Academic programmes supporting research and workforce development in geological sciences, mineral resources, and earth systems. Why This Opportunity Matters The TNE Grants with India 2026–27 represent a major step forward in strengthening educational and innovation ties between the UK and India. The programme aims to: Build sustainable UK–India higher education partnerships Support industry-relevant academic programmes Encourage interdisciplinary innovation Create opportunities for students in emerging sectors Enhance graduate employability Strengthen academia-industry collaboration Develop future-ready talent pipelines By encouraging collaboration between universities and industry, the grants aim to ensure that newly developed programmes remain relevant to rapidly evolving global workforce demands. Academia-Industry Collaboration Requirement One of the defining features of this grant programme is its strong emphasis on industry engagement. Applications must involve a three-way partnership consisting of: One or more UK higher education institutions One or more Indian higher education institutions Industry partners from either the UK or India Industry partners may include: Startups Small and medium enterprises (SMEs) Large corporations Commercial organizations operating legally within their jurisdictions This collaboration model is intended to ensure that TNE programmes are practical, industry-informed, and aligned with real-world technological and economic needs. Who Can Apply? UK Institutions Eligible UK applicants include: Higher education providers with degree-awarding powers Indian Institutions Eligible Indian institutions include: Government-funded higher education institutions Privately funded recognized higher education institutions Universities recognized by UGC, AICTE, or other competent Indian authorities Institutes of Eminence Institutes of National Importance Industry Partners Eligible industry collaborators include: Commercial organizations Micro enterprises Small and medium enterprises Large companies compliant with local laws Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the following deadlines: Clarification deadline: 28 July 2026 Application submission deadline: 5 August 2026 Submission time: 11:59 a.m. BST Project commencement: 30 September 2026 Late submissions may not be accepted. Final Thoughts The British Council TNE Grants with India 2026–27 provide a valuable platform for universities and industry leaders to shape the future of global higher education through innovation-driven collaboration. As emerging technologies continue to redefine industries worldwide, this initiative offers institutions a chance to create globally relevant academic programmes that address future workforce challenges while strengthening UK–India educational ties. Institutions interested in transnational education, interdisciplinary innovation, and international academic partnerships should strongly consider applying before the deadline. Learn More Apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the British Council TNE Grants with India 2026–27. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: WIPO City of Innovation Awards 2026: Global Call for Cities Driving Innovation and Creativity
summary: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has officially opened applications for the prestigious WIPO City of Innovation Awards 2026, a newly launched global initiative designed to recognize visionary cities committed to fostering innovation, creativity, and community-driven transformation. Cities across all 194 WIPO Member States are invited to submit bold pledges demonstrating how they will champion innovation and creativity over a 12-month period. Unlike traditional awards that only celebrate past achievements, the WIPO City of Innovation Awards focus on future-oriented commitments and transformative ideas capable of shaping sustainable and inclusive urban development. The deadline to apply is June 30, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. (CEST). About the WIPO City of Innovation Awards The WIPO City of Innovation Awards were launched for the first time in 2026 by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), the United Nations agency responsible for promoting innovation and creativity through intellectual property systems worldwide. This award program seeks to identify and recognize cities that are actively building environments where creativity, innovation, entrepreneurship, technology, and citizen participation can thrive. The initiative acknowledges the important role city leaders play in shaping innovative urban ecosystems and empowering local communities to contribute to social, economic, and cultural progress. The award is open to: Cities of all sizes Municipal governments Urban innovation ecosystems Creative and technology-driven communities Innovation-focused city administrations WIPO encourages mayors, governors, local government authorities, innovators, startups, artists, and community organizations to collaborate in developing strong city pledges capable of inspiring long-term transformation. Why the WIPO City of Innovation Awards Matter Cities are increasingly becoming centers of technological advancement, entrepreneurship, sustainability, cultural development, and creative expression. Through this award, WIPO aims to spotlight municipalities that are taking bold steps toward: Encouraging innovation-driven economic growth Empowering local creators and entrepreneurs Building sustainable urban futures Enhancing citizen participation Creating inclusive innovation ecosystems Promoting intellectual property awareness Strengthening local creative industries The award also serves as an international platform for sharing best practices and inspiring municipalities around the world to invest in innovation-led urban development. What Winning Cities Receive Selected cities will receive a comprehensive international recognition package designed to maximize visibility and showcase their innovation commitments globally. 1. Official WIPO Trophy Winning cities will receive a specially crafted symbolic WIPO City of Innovation trophy recognizing their commitment to urban transformation and creative leadership. 2. Global Recognition and International Visibility Award-winning cities will gain: International exposure through WIPO platforms Recognition during the official award ceremony in Geneva Networking opportunities with global policymakers Connections with innovation leaders and the international intellectual property community 3. Sponsored International Participation Travel expenses for one official city representative will be fully covered, enabling participation in: The official award ceremony High-level international networking events Meetings with mayors and diplomatic representatives from around the world 4. Professional Media and Storytelling Support WIPO’s professional media team will collaborate with winning cities to: Document innovation initiatives Showcase progress on city pledges Produce professional visual and media content Feature successful projects on selected WIPO platforms This provides cities with valuable international promotional exposure. 5. Permanent City Recognition Winning cities will receive the exclusive right to display the official commemorative WIPO City of Innovation plaque within their municipality. WIPO will provide the design specifications while cities maintain full control over installation and placement. Eligibility Criteria The WIPO City of Innovation Awards are open to cities located within WIPO Member States. Key eligibility conditions include: Applications are open to cities across all 194 WIPO Member States A maximum of three cities per country may apply Cities of all population sizes are encouraged to participate Applications must include a 12-month innovation and creativity pledge Applications must be approved and signed by an authorized city representative Authorized signatories may include: Mayors Governors Heads of city administrations Other authorized municipal officials Required Innovation Pledge Applicants must submit a clear and ambitious pledge describing how their city will promote innovation and creativity over the next 12 months. The pledge should explain: The city’s innovation vision Planned initiatives and programs Expected community impact Strategies for citizen engagement Long-term sustainability plans How innovation and creativity will be nurtured locally WIPO will evaluate the clarity, feasibility, originality, and long-term impact potential of each pledge. Application Process for the WIPO City of Innovation Awards 2026 Cities interested in applying must follow a structured multi-step application process. Step 1: Complete the Online Application Form Applicants must complete the official online application form in one of the six United Nations languages: English Arabic Chinese French Russian Spanish Step 2: Obtain Official Authorization The application form must be signed by a duly authorized city representative. Step 3: Submit Through Official Diplomatic Channels A PDF copy of the completed application form must be submitted to WIPO through: The country’s Permanent Mission to the United Nations Office at Geneva, or The Ministry of Foreign Affairs if no Permanent Mission exists Important Deadline Application Deadline: June 30, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. (CEST) Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit early to allow adequate processing time through diplomatic channels. WIPO City of Innovation Awards Timeline Applications Open April 1, 2026 Applications Close June 30, 2026 Announcement of Finalists September 21, 2026 An independent international jury panel will evaluate shortlisted applications based on the official selection criteria. Award Ceremony October 12, 2026 The winners will be announced during an official ceremony at WIPO headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland, alongside the Sixth Meeting of the UN Forum of Mayors. Up to three cities may be selected each year. Understanding the WIPO City of Innovation Trophy The WIPO City of Innovation trophy symbolizes the transformative power of cities through the elements of fire and water. According to WIPO: Fire represents technological ingenuity and the spark of innovation Water symbolizes creativity’s ability to adapt, overcome barriers, and shape communities over time The natural stone base reflects the enduring strength and resilience of cities Together, these elements embody the fusion of innovation, creativity, transformation, and sustainable urban progress. Why Cities Should Apply Participating in the WIPO City of Innovation Awards offers cities a rare opportunity to: Gain global visibility Showcase innovation leadership Build international partnerships Attract investment and collaboration Strengthen local creative ecosystems Inspire civic participation Promote sustainable urban growth The award also provides long-term reputational benefits by positioning cities as international leaders in innovation and creativity. About WIPO The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to building a balanced and effective global intellectual property ecosystem. WIPO works to: Promote innovation worldwide Support creativity and entrepreneurship Encourage sustainable development Strengthen intellectual property systems internationally Through initiatives like the WIPO City of Innovation Awards, the organization seeks to encourage local governments to become catalysts for creative and technological transformation. Learn More Apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the WIPO City of Innovation Awards 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post WIPO City of Innovation Awards 2026: Global Call for Cities Driving Innovation and Creativity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
